description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the debian:bullseye Docker image. Define a global before_script that runs apt-get update && apt-get -qy install fdroidserver wget --no-install-recommends, decodes $fdroid_keystore_base64 into keystore.bks, sets chmod 600 on fdroid/config.py and keystore.bks, and creates fdroid/repo. Add a build_repo job in the build stage that executes (cd fdroid/repo && while read -r line || [ -n "$line" ]; do rm -f $line; done < ../../cleanup.txt), ./download.py, cd fdroid, echo "keypass=\"$fdroid_keypass\"" >>config.py, echo "keystorepass=\"$fdroid_keystorepass\"" >>config.py, yamllint metadata/, fdroid update, rm -rf archive, mkdir /fdroid && fdroid deploy, rm -rf /fdroid/archive, cd .. && mkdir public && mv -v /fdroid public, echo "Repo size:" && du -h -d 3 public, ls -al public/fdroid/repo/, and a size check that fails if the repo exceeds 1 GB; always publish the public directory as an artifact. Add a pages job in the deploy stage that runs echo "publish to Gitlab pages", caches the fdroid directory, stores the public directory as a pages artifact that expires in 4 weeks, and runs only on the master branch or scheduled pipelines. Use the two stages build and deploy.
